title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Easy Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) at home! This easy recipe is packed with fresh seafood and has a perfectly crispy texture. Perfect appetizer!
longTailKeywords: how to make Korean seafood pancake
best Korean seafood pancake at home
lowCompetitionKeywords: Korean green onion pancake with seafood
seo.h1: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon)?
How to Make Korean Seafood Pancake: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Perfect Crispy Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: A freshly cooked Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) garnished with green onions and served with a soy dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the flavors of Korea with this homemade Korean Seafood Pancake, a delightful appetizer or light meal.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, celebrated for its crispy edges and tender, flavorful interior packed with fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. This classic dish is a delightful blend of textures and tastes, perfect as an appetizer or a light meal. Enjoy the authentic flavors of Korea right in your home with this satisfying recipe.

ingredients.notes: shrimp, squid, mussels, oysters, cut into bite-sized pieces
about 10-12 stalks, cut into 3-4 inch lengths

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Thoroughly clean and prepare all your seafood, cutting larger pieces like squid into bite-sized rings or strips. Pat the seafood dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture. Wash and trim the green onions, cutting them into 3-4 inch long pieces. Slice the yellow onion and julienne the carrot if using. Set aside.

instructions.title: Make the Pancake Batter
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour (if using), salt, and black pepper. Gradually add the cold water, whisking continuously until a smooth batter forms. Make sure there are no lumps. In a separate small bowl, lightly beat the two eggs.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Add the prepared seafood, green onions, sliced yellow onion, and julienned carrot (if using) into the batter. Gently mix until all ingredients are evenly coated. Be careful not to overmix, as this can make the pancake tough.

instructions.title: Preheat Pan and Add Batter
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add about 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil, swirling to coat the bottom of the pan. Once the oil is hot and shimmering, pour about half of the batter mixture into the pan, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake. Arrange the green onion pieces nicely across the top.

instructions.title: Cook the First Side
instructions.text: Let the pancake cook for about 3-4 minutes until the edges turn golden brown and crispy, and the bottom is lightly browned. While it cooks, drizzle half of the beaten egg mixture over the top of the pancake. This helps bind the ingredients and adds richness.

instructions.title: Flip and Cook Second Side
instructions.text: Carefully flip the pancake using a wide spatula. Add another tablespoon of vegetable oil around the edges of the pan to help crisp up the second side. Cook for another 3-4 minutes, pressing down gently with the spatula occasionally, until the second side is golden brown and the seafood is fully cooked. Remove from pan and place on a cutting board.

instructions.title: Prepare Dipping Sauce and Serve
instructions.text: While the second pancake cooks (repeat steps 4-6 for the remaining batter), prepare the dipping sauce by combining soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, toasted sesame seeds, and red chili flakes in a small bowl. Cut the cooked Haemul Pajeon into wedges or squares and serve immediately with the dipping sauce on the side.

tips: For an extra crispy pancake, use very cold water for the batter and add a tablespoon of potato starch or cornstarch along with the flours.
Ensure your seafood is well-drained and patted dry before adding to the batter to prevent a soggy pancake. Do not overmix the batter.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking to enjoy its optimal crispy texture. It pairs wonderfully with a simple soy-vinegar dipping sauce.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to Korea, where the vibrant flavors and comforting textures of traditional dishes captivate palates worldwide. Among these treasures, one stands out for its irresistible combination of fresh seafood, crisp vegetables, and a perfectly golden-brown crust: Haemul Pajeon, often known as Korean Seafood Pancake. This savory sensation is more than just a dish; it's a celebration of taste, texture, and communal dining, perfect for sharing with loved ones or enjoying as a delightful treat. Whether you're new to Korean cuisine or a seasoned fan, learning to make this iconic pancake will undoubtedly elevate your home cooking repertoire.

## What is Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon)?

Haemul Pajeon translates directly to "seafood scallion pancake," a name that perfectly encapsulates its essence. "Haemul" means seafood, and "Pajeon" refers to a scallion pancake. Unlike the sweet, fluffy pancakes many are familiar with, Haemul Pajeon is a savory delight, characterized by a thin, crispy exterior giving way to a soft, flavorful interior packed with tender seafood and aromatic green onions. It’s a staple at Korean restaurants, often enjoyed alongside a glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or cold beer, especially on a rainy day – a tradition believed to have originated from the sizzling sound of the pancake mimicking raindrops.

The beauty of this Korean Seafood Pancake lies in its harmonious blend of ingredients. Fresh scallions provide a pungent, slightly sweet base, while an assortment of seafood adds briny depth and varied textures. The batter, typically a mix of all-purpose flour, rice flour, and sometimes cornstarch, is crucial for achieving that coveted crispy crust. Each bite offers a satisfying crunch followed by a tender chew, making it an incredibly addictive and comforting dish.

## Ingredients for Haemul Pajeon

Creating an authentic Haemul Pajeon starts with fresh, quality ingredients. Here's what you'll typically need for this delightful Korean Seafood Pancake:

For the Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour
*   1/4 cup rice flour (for extra crispiness)
*   1 tablespoon cornstarch (optional, for even more crispiness)
*   1 large egg
*   1 to 1.5 cups very cold water (or anchovy broth for deeper flavor)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt

For the Fillings:
*   2 bunches green onions (about 10-12 stalks), cut into 2-3 inch lengths
*   1/2 cup mixed seafood, such as small shrimp, squid rings, mussels, and/or oysters (fresh or frozen, thawed and well-drained)
*   1/4 small onion, thinly sliced
*   1/4 cup other vegetables (optional), like julienned carrots or red bell pepper
*   1-2 fresh red or green chili peppers, thinly sliced (optional, for a kick)

For Cooking:
*   Vegetable oil or neutral cooking oil, generous amount

For the Dipping Sauce (basic):
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   Pinch of red chili flakes (gochugaru, optional)
*   1 teaspoon chopped green onion

## How to Make Korean Seafood Pancake: Step-by-Step

Making Haemul Pajeon at home is surprisingly straightforward, yielding impressive results. Follow these steps for a perfect Korean Seafood Pancake:

1.  **Prepare the Fillings:** Wash and thoroughly dry your green onions, then cut them into 2-3 inch segments. Thinly slice the onion and any other optional vegetables. If using frozen seafood, thaw it completely and pat it very dry with paper towels to prevent the pancake from becoming soggy. Slice larger pieces of seafood, like squid, into bite-sized pieces.

2.  **Mix the Batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, cornstarch (if using), and salt. In a separate bowl, whisk the egg with the cold water or broth. Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, mixing until just combined. A few lumps are okay; overmixing will develop the gluten and make the pancake tough. The batter should be thin enough to pour but thick enough to coat the ingredients. If it's too thick, add a little more cold water, a tablespoon at a time.

3.  **Assemble the Pancake:** Heat a large non-stick pan or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat. Add a generous amount of cooking oil (about 2-3 tablespoons) and swirl to coat the bottom. When the oil is hot, pour about half a cup of batter into the pan, swirling to create a thin, even layer.

4.  **Add Fillings:** Immediately arrange a handful of green onion segments evenly over the batter. Distribute a quarter of your mixed seafood, sliced onion, and any other vegetables or chili peppers over the green onions. Pour a very thin layer of additional batter over the toppings to help bind them.

5.  **Cook to Perfection:** Cook for 3-5 minutes on the first side, until the edges are golden brown and crispy and the batter is mostly set. Using a large spatula, carefully flip the pancake. Add a little more oil around the edges of the pan if it seems dry. Press down gently on the pancake to ensure even cooking and crispiness. Cook for another 3-5 minutes, or until the second side is golden brown and the seafood is cooked through.

6.  **Serve:** Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Slice it into wedges or squares and serve immediately while hot. Repeat the process with the remaining batter and fillings to make more pancakes.

## Tips for the Perfect Crispy Pajeon

Achieving that signature crispiness is key to a truly outstanding Korean Seafood Pancake. Here are some tips to help you master it:

*   **Cold Batter is Crucial:** Using very cold water (or even adding an ice cube to the batter) helps inhibit gluten development, resulting in a crispier texture when fried.
*   **Don't Skimp on Oil:** Pajeon is fried, not just pan-seared. A generous amount of oil creates a beautifully crispy crust.
*   **Rice Flour and Cornstarch:** These flours are your secret weapons for crispiness. Rice flour lends a delicate crunch, while cornstarch helps achieve a light, airy crispness.
*   **Thin is In:** Aim for a relatively thin pancake. A thick pancake can be doughy in the center.
*   **High Heat, Then Medium:** Start with medium-high heat to get a good sizzle and initial crisping, then reduce to medium to ensure the inside cooks through without burning the outside.
*   **Pat Seafood Dry:** Excess moisture from seafood can make your pancake soggy. Always pat thawed seafood very dry before adding it to the batter.

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce

Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed fresh off the griddle. Its savory, slightly briny flavors are beautifully complemented by a simple yet zesty dipping sauce.

**Basic Dipping Sauce:** Whisk together 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 1 tablespoon rice vinegar, 1 teaspoon sesame oil, 1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds, a pinch of red chili flakes (gochugaru), and 1 teaspoon of finely chopped green onion. You can adjust the proportions to your taste. Some like to add a tiny bit of sugar or a clove of minced garlic for extra flavor.

**Pairings:** In Korea, Haemul Pajeon is famously paired with makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or a cold beer, especially on a rainy day. It also makes an excellent appetizer for a larger Korean meal, a satisfying snack, or a light lunch. Serve it alongside other popular Korean banchan (side dishes) like kimchi or pickled radishes for a complete experience.

## Variations and Add-ins

While the classic Korean Seafood Pancake is perfect as is, it's also wonderfully versatile. Don't hesitate to experiment with different fillings to suit your preferences:

*   **Kimchi Pajeon:** A very popular variation where chopped kimchi is incorporated into the batter or scattered over the pancake. The fermented spice of kimchi adds a fantastic depth of flavor.
*   **Vegetarian Pajeon:** Skip the seafood entirely and load up on vegetables. Sliced mushrooms (shiitake, oyster, enoki), zucchini, bell peppers, or even finely shredded potato can make a delicious vegetarian option.
*   **Meat Pajeon:** Thinly sliced beef (like bulgogi meat) or pork can be added for a heartier pancake. Marinate the meat briefly for extra flavor.
*   **Cheese Pajeon:** For a modern twist, sprinkle some shredded mozzarella cheese over the pancake during the last few minutes of cooking, allowing it to melt and get slightly crispy.
*   **Spicy Pajeon:** Increase the amount of chili peppers or add a dollop of gochujang (Korean chili paste) to the batter for a fiery kick.

Haemul Pajeon is a testament to the comforting and rich flavors of Korean cuisine. Its delightful combination of textures and tastes makes it a universally appealing dish, perfect for any occasion. By following these tips and steps, you can recreate the magic of this beloved Korean Seafood Pancake right in your own kitchen, bringing a piece of Korea's culinary heart to your table. So gather your ingredients, fire up your pan, and get ready to enjoy a truly authentic and delicious experience!
